evals.prompt: Set up a new Python library named `geo-normalizer` with a modern `pyproject.toml`, `src/` layout, Ruff, pytest, mypy, pre-commit, and GitHub Actions.
evals.expected_output: Uses the project-setup skill and produces a maintainable package scaffold with modern defaults.

evals.prompt: I have a Python package with weak typing and messy exception handling. Clean it up with Ruff and mypy settings and show the refactor plan.
evals.expected_output: Uses the code-quality skill and recommends concrete linting, typing, and refactoring changes.

evals.prompt: Add a Click-based CLI to this Python library with subcommands, stdin support, and tests using CliRunner.
evals.expected_output: Uses the cli-development skill and includes entry points, command structure, and CLI tests.

evals.prompt: We are about to publish version 1.4.0 of our Python package. Help me decide the version bump, update the changelog, tag the release, and wire up PyPI trusted publishing.
evals.expected_output: Uses the packaging and release-management skills and gives a coherent release workflow.

evals.prompt: Review this Python library for security and test quality. I want to know what is missing before we make it public.
evals.expected_output: Uses the security-audit, testing-strategy, and library-review skills to produce a structured audit.

evals.prompt: Our Python package has almost no docs. Add Google-style docstrings, a README outline, and a Sphinx starter config.
evals.expected_output: Uses the documentation skill and returns concrete documentation assets and structure.
